In 1581 (Tenshō 9), {{ill|Nose Yoritugu|ja|能勢頼次|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Yoritugu|qq=}}, after being defeated by [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]], retreated to Daikū-ji’s remains and built {{ill|Iraku Mountain Castle|ja|為楽山城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iraku Mountain Castle|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Following the [[:en:Honnō-ji Incident|Honnō-ji Incident]], Yoritugu joined [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s enemies and fled, losing his domain. In 1600 (Keichō 5), Yoritugu regained Nose under [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and was reinstated as a Tokugawa vassal. Yoritugu became a devout Nichiren follower under {{ill|Nikken|ja|日乾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikken|qq=}}, the 21st chief abbot of {{ill|Minobusan Kuon-ji|ja|身延山久遠寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobusan Kuon-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久遠寺|1=Kuon-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1516881}}, and established Shinjō-ji in 1603 (Keichō 8). A new Myōken image was carved and enshrined in the newly constructed {{ill|Ka'un-den|ja|開運殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ka'un-den|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the former castle site, renaming the mountain to Myōken Mountain. == Grounds == The temple features numerous notable structures: * '''Ka'un-den (Main Hall)''' – Rebuilt in 1787 (Tenmei 7) through the patronage of Nose Yoritada. * '''Seirei (Faith Hall)''' – Built in 1998, featuring a glass-floored worship area. * '''Sōshidō (Founder’s Hall)''' – Renovated in 1896. * '''Jōsuidō (Pure Water Hall)''' – Donated by 4th-generation Nakamura Utaemon. * '''Ema-dō (Votive Tablet Hall)''' – Built in 1797 (Kansei 8). * '''Treasure Hall''' – Built in 1922. * '''Statues of Nikken and Nose Yoritugu'''. == Access == While historically accessible by {{ill|Nose Highway|lt=Nose Highway|lt=Nose Kaidō|ja|能勢街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Kaidō|qq=}}, modern visitors must rely on private transport or hiking, as public transit access ceased in 2023. Parking is available near the summit, and seasonal traffic restrictions may apply during major events such as New Year’s visits. Shuttle services are provided for worshippers from nearby transit points upon prior arrangement. == Cultural Properties == * '''Natural Monuments (Osaka Prefecture and Kawanishi City):''' ** Beech forest on Myōken Mountain.{{Cite web|url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/spot/8kt5o8vuo9/|title=妙見山のブナ林｜のせでん【能勢電鉄】|accessdate=2024-10-23|publisher=能勢電鉄}} == External Links == * [https://www.myoken.org/ Official Website] * {{Instagram|nosemyokenzan}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Nichiren-shū temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka]] }} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[fr:Nose Myōkenzan]] [[ja:能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)]] [[ja:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E8%83%BD%E5%8B%A2%E5%A6%99%E8%A6%8B%E5%B1%B1 (%E6%97%A5%E8%93%AE%E5%AE%97)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Buildings in Nose Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nōse]] {{Commons category|Nose Myokenzan}} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[zh:能勢妙見山]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)|version=103734152|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11611929}}

[[commons:Category:Nose Myokenzan]][[fr:Nose Myōkenzan]][[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]][[zh:能勢妙見山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11611929]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Nose Myōkenzan | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Nichiren Shū|qid=Q206611}} |image = File:Nose Myokenzan kaiunden.jpg | location = 661 Nomachū, Nose-chō, Toyono-gun, Osaka, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.9288315|N|135.466687|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mount = Murozan | sect = Nichiren | deity = {{ill|Myōken|qid=Q10960623}} | established = Keichō 8 (1603) | founder = Nikken | main founder = Nose Yoritugu | alias = Nose Myōkendō
Nose Myōken-gū | official_name = Murozan Shinjō-ji Detached Temple Nose Myōkenzan | related_temples = [[Shinjō-ji (Nose)|Shinjō-ji]] | website = [https://www.myoken.org/ Official Website] | prefecture= {{ill|Nose|qid=Q1203349}} | founded_by= {{ill|Nikken|qid=Q11504819}}; {{ill|Q52083736|qid=Q52083736}} }} '''Nose Myōkenzan''' (能勢妙見山, {{lang-en-short|Nose Myōkenzan}}) is a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple located in Nose Town, Toyono District, Osaka, Japan. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Murozan. The main deity is [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. Situated near the summit of {{ill|Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)|lt=Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)|lt=Myōken Mountain (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mountain (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}}, the temple is a revered site for the North Star faith. == Overview == '''Nose Myōkendō''' (能勢妙見堂) or '''Nose Myōken-gū''' (能勢妙見宮), as it is sometimes called, reflects the influence of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], as evidenced by the presence of a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate at the temple's entrance.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kawanishi.hyogo.jp/dbps_data/_material_/localhost/00test/myoukenryousen.pdf#page=3|title=妙見稜線コース（川西市ハイキングコース案内）|date=2018-03-06|accessdate=2024-10-23}} The temple’s full name is '''Murozan Shinjō-ji Detached Temple Nose Myōkenzan'''.{{Cite web|url=https://www.myoken.org/omen/|title=能勢妙見山の正式name|publisher=Nichiren宗霊場 能勢妙見山|accessdate=2024-10-23}} It is a {{ill|detached temple|ja|境外仏堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/detached temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏堂|1=dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377515}} of {{ill|Shinjō-ji (Nose)|ja|真如寺 (大阪府能勢町)|lt=Shinjō-ji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō-ji (Nose)|qq=|1=Shinnyo-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496389}}, also located in Nose Town. Although technically subordinate to Shinjō-ji, Nose Myōkenzan attracts far more visitors and holds a significant position among Nichiren temples in the Kansai region. Initially managed directly by Shinjō-ji’s priests, it was designated as a detached temple under the [[:en:Religious Organizations Law|Religious Organizations Law (Japan)]] in 1941. Notably, a nearby temple, {{ill|Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki-ji|qq=}}, which also enshrines Myōken Bosatsu, is unrelated to Nose Myōkenzan and belongs to the {{ill|Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken sect|ja|妙見宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken sect|qq=}} of the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] school. == History == The origins of the temple date back to the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō-shōhō]] era (749–757) when the monk Gyōki established {{ill|Daikū-ji|ja|大空寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikū-ji|qq=|1=Daikū-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97377211}} on the summit of Iraku Mountain (now Myōken Mountain).{{Cite web|url=https://smtrc.jp/town-archives/city/toyonaka/p02.html|title=能勢氏ゆかりのNichiren宗霊場「能勢妙見山」|publisher=三井住友トラスト不動産|accessdate=2024-10-23}} During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], in 986 (Kanna 2), [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]], a devout follower of Myōken Bosatsu, relocated an image of {{ill|Chintaku Reifu|lt=Chintaku Reifu Shin|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮宅霊符}} (a representation of Myōken) to this site. Later, Mitsunaka’s grandson, {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni|ja|源頼国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yorikuni|qq=}}, settled in the area, established the Nose clan, and continued to venerate the deity. In 1581 (Tenshō 9), {{ill|Nose Yoritugu|ja|能勢頼次|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Yoritugu|qq=}}, after being defeated by [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]], retreated to Daikū-ji’s remains and built {{ill|Iraku Mountain Castle|ja|為楽山城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iraku Mountain Castle|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Following the [[:en:Honnō-ji Incident|Honnō-ji Incident]], Yoritugu joined [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s enemies and fled, losing his domain. In 1600 (Keichō 5), Yoritugu regained Nose under [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and was reinstated as a Tokugawa vassal. Yoritugu became a devout Nichiren follower under {{ill|Nikken|ja|日乾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikken|qq=}}, the 21st chief abbot of {{ill|Minobusan Kuon-ji|ja|身延山久遠寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobusan Kuon-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久遠寺|1=Kuon-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1516881}}, and established Shinjō-ji in 1603 (Keichō 8). A new Myōken image was carved and enshrined in the newly constructed {{ill|Ka'un-den|ja|開運殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ka'un-den|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the former castle site, renaming the mountain to Myōken Mountain. == Grounds == The temple features numerous notable structures: * '''Ka'un-den (Main Hall)''' – Rebuilt in 1787 (Tenmei 7) through the patronage of Nose Yoritada. * '''Seirei (Faith Hall)''' – Built in 1998, featuring a glass-floored worship area. * '''Sōshidō (Founder’s Hall)''' – Renovated in 1896. * '''Jōsuidō (Pure Water Hall)''' – Donated by 4th-generation Nakamura Utaemon. * '''Ema-dō (Votive Tablet Hall)''' – Built in 1797 (Kansei 8). * '''Treasure Hall''' – Built in 1922. * '''Statues of Nikken and Nose Yoritugu'''. == Access == While historically accessible by {{ill|Nose Highway|lt=Nose Highway|lt=Nose Kaidō|ja|能勢街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Kaidō|qq=}}, modern visitors must rely on private transport or hiking, as public transit access ceased in 2023. Parking is available near the summit, and seasonal traffic restrictions may apply during major events such as New Year’s visits. Shuttle services are provided for worshippers from nearby transit points upon prior arrangement. == Cultural Properties == * '''Natural Monuments (Osaka Prefecture and Kawanishi City):''' ** Beech forest on Myōken Mountain.{{Cite web|url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/spot/8kt5o8vuo9/|title=妙見山のブナ林｜のせでん【能勢電鉄】|accessdate=2024-10-23|publisher=能勢電鉄}} == External Links == * [https://www.myoken.org/ Official Website] * {{Instagram|nosemyokenzan}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Nichiren-shū temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka]] }} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[fr:Nose Myōkenzan]] [[ja:能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)]] [[ja:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E8%83%BD%E5%8B%A2%E5%A6%99%E8%A6%8B%E5%B1%B1 (%E6%97%A5%E8%93%AE%E5%AE%97)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Buildings in Nose Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nōse]] {{Commons category|Nose Myokenzan}} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[zh:能勢妙見山]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)|version=103734152|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11611929}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Nose Myokenzan]][[fr:Nose Myōkenzan]][[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]][[zh:能勢妙見山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11611929]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Nose Myōkenzan | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Nichiren Shū|WD=Q206611}} |image = File:Nose Myokenzan kaiunden.jpg | location = 661 Nomachū, Nose-chō, Toyono-gun, Osaka, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.9288315|N|135.466687|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mount = Murozan | sect = Nichiren | deity = {{ill|Myōken|WD=Q10960623}} | established = Keichō 8 (1603) | founder = Nikken | main founder = Nose Yoritugu | alias = Nose Myōkendō
Nose Myōken-gū | official_name = Murozan Shinjō-ji Detached Temple Nose Myōkenzan | related_temples = [[Shinjō-ji (Nose)|Shinjō-ji]] | website = [https://www.myoken.org/ Official Website] | prefecture= {{ill|Nose|WD=Q1203349}} | founded_by= {{ill|Nikken|WD=Q11504819}}; {{ill|Q52083736|WD=Q52083736}} }} '''Nose Myōkenzan''' (能勢妙見山, {{lang-en-short|Nose Myōkenzan}}) is a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple located in Nose Town, Toyono District, Osaka, Japan. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Murozan. The main deity is [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. Situated near the summit of {{ill|Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)|lt=Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)|lt=Myōken Mountain (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mountain (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}}, the temple is a revered site for the North Star faith. == Overview == '''Nose Myōkendō''' (能勢妙見堂) or '''Nose Myōken-gū''' (能勢妙見宮), as it is sometimes called, reflects the influence of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], as evidenced by the presence of a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate at the temple's entrance.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kawanishi.hyogo.jp/dbps_data/_material_/localhost/00test/myoukenryousen.pdf#page=3|title=妙見稜線コース（川西市ハイキングコース案内）|date=2018-03-06|accessdate=2024-10-23}} The temple’s full name is '''Murozan Shinjō-ji Detached Temple Nose Myōkenzan'''.{{Cite web|url=https://www.myoken.org/omen/|title=能勢妙見山の正式name|publisher=Nichiren宗霊場 能勢妙見山|accessdate=2024-10-23}} It is a {{ill|detached temple|ja|境外仏堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/detached temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏堂|1=dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377515}} of {{ill|Shinjō-ji (Nose)|ja|真如寺 (大阪府能勢町)|lt=Shinjō-ji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō-ji (Nose)|qq=|1=Shinnyo-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496389}}, also located in Nose Town. Although technically subordinate to Shinjō-ji, Nose Myōkenzan attracts far more visitors and holds a significant position among Nichiren temples in the Kansai region. Initially managed directly by Shinjō-ji’s priests, it was designated as a detached temple under the [[:en:Religious Organizations Law|Religious Organizations Law (Japan)]] in 1941. Notably, a nearby temple, {{ill|Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki-ji|qq=}}, which also enshrines Myōken Bosatsu, is unrelated to Nose Myōkenzan and belongs to the {{ill|Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken sect|ja|妙見宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken sect|qq=}} of the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] school. == History == The origins of the temple date back to the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō-shōhō]] era (749–757) when the monk Gyōki established {{ill|Daikū-ji|ja|大空寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikū-ji|qq=|1=Daikū-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97377211}} on the summit of Iraku Mountain (now Myōken Mountain).{{Cite web|url=https://smtrc.jp/town-archives/city/toyonaka/p02.html|title=能勢氏ゆかりのNichiren宗霊場「能勢妙見山」|publisher=三井住友トラスト不動産|accessdate=2024-10-23}} During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], in 986 (Kanna 2), [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]], a devout follower of Myōken Bosatsu, relocated an image of {{ill|Chintaku Reifu|lt=Chintaku Reifu Shin|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮宅霊符}} (a representation of Myōken) to this site. Later, Mitsunaka’s grandson, {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni|ja|源頼国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yorikuni|qq=}}, settled in the area, established the Nose clan, and continued to venerate the deity. In 1581 (Tenshō 9), {{ill|Nose Yoritugu|ja|能勢頼次|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Yoritugu|qq=}}, after being defeated by [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]], retreated to Daikū-ji’s remains and built {{ill|Iraku Mountain Castle|ja|為楽山城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iraku Mountain Castle|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Following the [[:en:Honnō-ji Incident|Honnō-ji Incident]], Yoritugu joined [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s enemies and fled, losing his domain. In 1600 (Keichō 5), Yoritugu regained Nose under [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and was reinstated as a Tokugawa vassal. Yoritugu became a devout Nichiren follower under {{ill|Nikken|ja|日乾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikken|qq=}}, the 21st chief abbot of {{ill|Minobusan Kuon-ji|ja|身延山久遠寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobusan Kuon-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久遠寺|1=Kuon-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1516881}}, and established Shinjō-ji in 1603 (Keichō 8). A new Myōken image was carved and enshrined in the newly constructed {{ill|Ka'un-den|ja|開運殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ka'un-den|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the former castle site, renaming the mountain to Myōken Mountain. == Grounds == The temple features numerous notable structures: * '''Ka'un-den (Main Hall)''' – Rebuilt in 1787 (Tenmei 7) through the patronage of Nose Yoritada. * '''Seirei (Faith Hall)''' – Built in 1998, featuring a glass-floored worship area. * '''Sōshidō (Founder’s Hall)''' – Renovated in 1896. * '''Jōsuidō (Pure Water Hall)''' – Donated by 4th-generation Nakamura Utaemon. * '''Ema-dō (Votive Tablet Hall)''' – Built in 1797 (Kansei 8). * '''Treasure Hall''' – Built in 1922. * '''Statues of Nikken and Nose Yoritugu'''. == Access == While historically accessible by {{ill|Nose Highway|lt=Nose Highway|lt=Nose Kaidō|ja|能勢街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Kaidō|qq=}}, modern visitors must rely on private transport or hiking, as public transit access ceased in 2023. Parking is available near the summit, and seasonal traffic restrictions may apply during major events such as New Year’s visits. Shuttle services are provided for worshippers from nearby transit points upon prior arrangement. == Cultural Properties == * '''Natural Monuments (Osaka Prefecture and Kawanishi City):''' ** Beech forest on Myōken Mountain.{{Cite web|url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/spot/8kt5o8vuo9/|title=妙見山のブナ林｜のせでん【能勢電鉄】|accessdate=2024-10-23|publisher=能勢電鉄}} == External Links == * [https://www.myoken.org/ Official Website] * {{Instagram|nosemyokenzan}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Nichiren-shū temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka]] }} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[fr:Nose Myōkenzan]] [[ja:能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)]] [[ja:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E8%83%BD%E5%8B%A2%E5%A6%99%E8%A6%8B%E5%B1%B1 (%E6%97%A5%E8%93%AE%E5%AE%97)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Buildings in Nose Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nōse]] {{Commons category|Nose Myokenzan}} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[zh:能勢妙見山]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)|version=103734152|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11611929}}

Although technically subordinate to Shinjō-ji, Nose Myōkenzan attracts far more visitors and holds a significant position among Nichiren temples in the Kansai region. Initially managed directly by Shinjō-ji’s priests, it was designated as a detached temple under the [[:en:Religious Organizations Law|Religious Organizations Law (Japan)]] in 1941. Notably, a nearby temple, {{ill|Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki-ji|qq=}}, which also enshrines Myōken Bosatsu, is unrelated to Nose Myōkenzan and belongs to the {{ill|Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken sect|ja|妙見宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken sect|qq=}} of the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] school. == History == The origins of the temple date back to the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō-shōhō]] era (749–757) when the monk Gyōki established {{ill|Daikū-ji|ja|大空寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikū-ji|qq=|1=Daikū-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97377211}} on the summit of Iraku Mountain (now Myōken Mountain).{{Cite web|url=https://smtrc.jp/town-archives/city/toyonaka/p02.html|title=能勢氏ゆかりのNichiren宗霊場「能勢妙見山」|publisher=三井住友トラスト不動産|accessdate=2024-10-23}} During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], in 986 (Kanna 2), [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]], a devout follower of Myōken Bosatsu, relocated an image of {{ill|Chintaku Reifu|lt=Chintaku Reifu Shin|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮宅霊符}} (a representation of Myōken) to this site. Later, Mitsunaka’s grandson, {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni|ja|源頼国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yorikuni|qq=}}, settled in the area, established the Nose clan, and continued to venerate the deity. In 1581 (Tenshō 9), {{ill|Nose Yoritugu|ja|能勢頼次|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Yoritugu|qq=}}, after being defeated by [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]], retreated to Daikū-ji’s remains and built {{ill|Iraku Mountain Castle|ja|為楽山城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iraku Mountain Castle|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Following the [[:en:Honnō-ji Incident|Honnō-ji Incident]], Yoritugu joined [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s enemies and fled, losing his domain. In 1600 (Keichō 5), Yoritugu regained Nose under [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and was reinstated as a Tokugawa vassal. Yoritugu became a devout Nichiren follower under {{ill|Nikken|ja|日乾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikken|qq=}}, the 21st chief abbot of {{ill|Minobusan Kuon-ji|ja|身延山久遠寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobusan Kuon-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久遠寺|1=Kuon-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1516881}}, and established Shinjō-ji in 1603 (Keichō 8). A new Myōken image was carved and enshrined in the newly constructed {{ill|Ka'un-den|ja|開運殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ka'un-den|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the former castle site, renaming the mountain to Myōken Mountain. == Grounds == The temple features numerous notable structures: * '''Ka'un-den (Main Hall)''' – Rebuilt in 1787 (Tenmei 7) through the patronage of Nose Yoritada. * '''Seirei (Faith Hall)''' – Built in 1998, featuring a glass-floored worship area. * '''Sōshidō (Founder’s Hall)''' – Renovated in 1896. * '''Jōsuidō (Pure Water Hall)''' – Donated by 4th-generation Nakamura Utaemon. * '''Ema-dō (Votive Tablet Hall)''' – Built in 1797 (Kansei 8). * '''Treasure Hall''' – Built in 1922. * '''Statues of Nikken and Nose Yoritugu'''. == Access == While historically accessible by {{ill|Nose Highway|lt=Nose Highway|lt=Nose Kaidō|ja|能勢街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Kaidō|qq=}}, modern visitors must rely on private transport or hiking, as public transit access ceased in 2023. Parking is available near the summit, and seasonal traffic restrictions may apply during major events such as New Year’s visits. Shuttle services are provided for worshippers from nearby transit points upon prior arrangement. == Cultural Properties == * '''Natural Monuments (Osaka Prefecture and Kawanishi City):''' ** Beech forest on Myōken Mountain.{{Cite web|url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/spot/8kt5o8vuo9/|title=妙見山のブナ林｜のせでん【能勢電鉄】|accessdate=2024-10-23|publisher=能勢電鉄}} == External Links == * [https://www.myoken.org/ Official Website] * {{Instagram|nosemyokenzan}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Nichiren-shū temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka]] }} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[fr:Nose Myōkenzan]] [[ja:能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)]] [[ja:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E8%83%BD%E5%8B%A2%E5%A6%99%E8%A6%8B%E5%B1%B1 (%E6%97%A5%E8%93%AE%E5%AE%97)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Buildings in Nose Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nōse]] {{Commons category|Nose Myokenzan}} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[zh:能勢妙見山]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)|version=103734152|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11611929}}

Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Murozan. The main deity is [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. Situated near the summit of {{ill|Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)|lt=Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)|lt=Myōken Mountain (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mountain (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}}, the temple is a revered site for the North Star faith. == Overview == '''Nose Myōkendō''' (能勢妙見堂) or '''Nose Myōken-gū''' (能勢妙見宮), as it is sometimes called, reflects the influence of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], as evidenced by the presence of a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate at the temple's entrance.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kawanishi.hyogo.jp/dbps_data/_material_/localhost/00test/myoukenryousen.pdf#page=3|title=妙見稜線コース（川西市ハイキングコース案内）|date=2018-03-06|accessdate=2024-10-23}} The temple’s full name is '''Murozan Shinjō-ji Detached Temple Nose Myōkenzan'''.{{Cite web|url=https://www.myoken.org/omen/|title=能勢妙見山の正式name|publisher=Nichiren宗霊場 能勢妙見山|accessdate=2024-10-23}} It is a {{ill|detached temple|ja|境外仏堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/detached temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏堂|1=dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377515}} of {{ill|Shinjō-ji (Nose)|ja|真如寺 (大阪府能勢町)|lt=Shinjō-ji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō-ji (Nose)|1=Shinnyo-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496389}}, also located in Nose Town. A new Myōken image was carved and enshrined in the newly constructed {{ill|Ka'un-den|ja|開運殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ka'un-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the former castle site, renaming the mountain to Myōken Mountain. == Grounds == The temple features numerous notable structures: * '''Ka'un-den (Main Hall)''' – Rebuilt in 1787 (Tenmei 7) through the patronage of Nose Yoritada. * '''Seirei (Faith Hall)''' – Built in 1998, featuring a glass-floored worship area. * '''Sōshidō (Founder’s Hall)''' – Renovated in 1896. * '''Jōsuidō (Pure Water Hall)''' – Donated by 4th-generation Nakamura Utaemon. * '''Ema-dō (Votive Tablet Hall)''' – Built in 1797 (Kansei 8). * '''Treasure Hall''' – Built in 1922. * '''Statues of Nikken and Nose Yoritugu'''. == Access == While historically accessible by {{ill|Nose Highway|lt=Nose Highway|lt=Nose Kaidō|ja|能勢街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Kaidō|qq=}}, modern visitors must rely on private transport or hiking, as public transit access ceased in 2023. Parking is available near the summit, and seasonal traffic restrictions may apply during major events such as New Year’s visits. Shuttle services are provided for worshippers from nearby transit points upon prior arrangement. == Cultural Properties == * '''Natural Monuments (Osaka Prefecture and Kawanishi City):''' ** Beech forest on Myōken Mountain.{{Cite web|url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/spot/8kt5o8vuo9/|title=妙見山のブナ林｜のせでん【能勢電鉄】|accessdate=2024-10-23|publisher=能勢電鉄}} == External Links == * [https://www.myoken.org/ Official Website] * {{Instagram|nosemyokenzan}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Nichiren-shū temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka]] }} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[fr:Nose Myōkenzan]] [[ja:能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)]] [[ja:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E8%83%BD%E5%8B%A2%E5%A6%99%E8%A6%8B%E5%B1%B1 (%E6%97%A5%E8%93%AE%E5%AE%97)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Buildings in Nose Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nōse]] {{Commons category|Nose Myokenzan}} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[zh:能勢妙見山]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)|version=103734152|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11611929}}

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Nose Myokenzan]][[fr:Nose Myōkenzan]][[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]][[zh:能勢妙見山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11611929]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Nose Myōkenzan | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Nichiren Shū|WD=Q206611}} |image = File:Nose Myokenzan kaiunden.jpg | location = 661 Nomachū, Nose-chō, Toyono-gun, Osaka, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.9288315|N|135.466687|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mount = Murozan | sect = Nichiren | deity = {{ill|Myōken|WD=Q10960623}} | established = Keichō 8 (1603) | founder = Nikken | main founder = Nose Yoritugu | alias = Nose Myōkendō
Nose Myōken-gū | official_name = Murozan Shinjō-ji Detached Temple Nose Myōkenzan | related_temples = [[Shinjō-ji (Nose)|Shinjō-ji]] | website = [https://www.myoken.org/ Official Website] | prefecture= {{ill|Nose|WD=Q1203349}} | founded_by= {{ill|Nikken|WD=Q11504819}}; {{ill|Q52083736|WD=Q52083736}} }} '''Nose Myōkenzan''' (能勢妙見山, {{lang-en-short|Nose Myōkenzan}}) is a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple located in Nose Town, Toyono District, Osaka, Japan. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Murozan. The main deity is [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. Situated near the summit of {{ill|Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)|lt=Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)|lt=Myōken Mountain (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mountain (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}}, the temple is a revered site for the North Star faith. == Overview == '''Nose Myōkendō''' (能勢妙見堂) or '''Nose Myōken-gū''' (能勢妙見宮), as it is sometimes called, reflects the influence of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], as evidenced by the presence of a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate at the temple's entrance.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kawanishi.hyogo.jp/dbps_data/_material_/localhost/00test/myoukenryousen.pdf#page=3|title=妙見稜線コース（川西市ハイキングコース案内）|date=2018-03-06|accessdate=2024-10-23}} The temple’s full name is '''Murozan Shinjō-ji Detached Temple Nose Myōkenzan'''.{{Cite web|url=https://www.myoken.org/omen/|title=能勢妙見山の正式name|publisher=Nichiren宗霊場 能勢妙見山|accessdate=2024-10-23}} It is a {{ill|detached temple|ja|境外仏堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/detached temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏堂|1=dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377515}} of {{ill|Shinjō-ji (Nose)|ja|真如寺 (大阪府能勢町)|lt=Shinjō-ji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō-ji (Nose)|1=Shinnyo-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496389}}, also located in Nose Town. Although technically subordinate to Shinjō-ji, Nose Myōkenzan attracts far more visitors and holds a significant position among Nichiren temples in the Kansai region. Initially managed directly by Shinjō-ji’s priests, it was designated as a detached temple under the [[:en:Religious Organizations Law|Religious Organizations Law (Japan)]] in 1941. Notably, a nearby temple, {{ill|Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki-ji|qq=}}, which also enshrines Myōken Bosatsu, is unrelated to Nose Myōkenzan and belongs to the {{ill|Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken sect|ja|妙見宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken sect|qq=}} of the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] school. == History == The origins of the temple date back to the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō-shōhō]] era (749–757) when the monk Gyōki established {{ill|Daikū-ji|ja|大空寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikū-ji|1=Daikū-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97377211}} on the summit of Iraku Mountain (now Myōken Mountain).{{Cite web|url=https://smtrc.jp/town-archives/city/toyonaka/p02.html|title=能勢氏ゆかりのNichiren宗霊場「能勢妙見山」|publisher=三井住友トラスト不動産|accessdate=2024-10-23}} During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], in 986 (Kanna 2), [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]], a devout follower of Myōken Bosatsu, relocated an image of {{ill|Chintaku Reifu|lt=Chintaku Reifu Shin|ja|鎮宅霊符神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮宅霊符}} (a representation of Myōken) to this site. Later, Mitsunaka’s grandson, {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni|ja|源頼国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yorikuni|qq=}}, settled in the area, established the Nose clan, and continued to venerate the deity. In 1581 (Tenshō 9), {{ill|Nose Yoritugu|ja|能勢頼次|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Yoritugu|qq=}}, after being defeated by [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]], retreated to Daikū-ji’s remains and built {{ill|Iraku Mountain Castle|ja|為楽山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iraku Mountain Castle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Following the [[:en:Honnō-ji Incident|Honnō-ji Incident]], Yoritugu joined [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s enemies and fled, losing his domain. In 1600 (Keichō 5), Yoritugu regained Nose under [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and was reinstated as a Tokugawa vassal. Yoritugu became a devout Nichiren follower under {{ill|Nikken|ja|日乾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikken|qq=}}, the 21st chief abbot of {{ill|Minobusan Kuon-ji|ja|身延山久遠寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobusan Kuon-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久遠寺|1=Kuon-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1516881}}, and established Shinjō-ji in 1603 (Keichō 8). A new Myōken image was carved and enshrined in the newly constructed {{ill|Ka'un-den|ja|開運殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ka'un-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the former castle site, renaming the mountain to Myōken Mountain. == Grounds == The temple features numerous notable structures: * '''Ka'un-den (Main Hall)''' – Rebuilt in 1787 (Tenmei 7) through the patronage of Nose Yoritada. * '''Seirei (Faith Hall)''' – Built in 1998, featuring a glass-floored worship area. * '''Sōshidō (Founder’s Hall)''' – Renovated in 1896. * '''Jōsuidō (Pure Water Hall)''' – Donated by 4th-generation Nakamura Utaemon. * '''Ema-dō (Votive Tablet Hall)''' – Built in 1797 (Kansei 8). * '''Treasure Hall''' – Built in 1922. * '''Statues of Nikken and Nose Yoritugu'''. == Access == While historically accessible by {{ill|Nose Highway|lt=Nose Highway|lt=Nose Kaidō|ja|能勢街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Kaidō|qq=}}, modern visitors must rely on private transport or hiking, as public transit access ceased in 2023. Parking is available near the summit, and seasonal traffic restrictions may apply during major events such as New Year’s visits. Shuttle services are provided for worshippers from nearby transit points upon prior arrangement. == Cultural Properties == * '''Natural Monuments (Osaka Prefecture and Kawanishi City):''' ** Beech forest on Myōken Mountain.{{Cite web|url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/spot/8kt5o8vuo9/|title=妙見山のブナ林｜のせでん【能勢電鉄】|accessdate=2024-10-23|publisher=能勢電鉄}} == External Links == * [https://www.myoken.org/ Official Website] * {{Instagram|nosemyokenzan}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Nichiren-shū temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka]] }} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[fr:Nose Myōkenzan]] [[ja:能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)]] [[ja:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E8%83%BD%E5%8B%A2%E5%A6%99%E8%A6%8B%E5%B1%B1 (%E6%97%A5%E8%93%AE%E5%AE%97)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Buildings in Nose Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nōse]] {{Commons category|Nose Myokenzan}} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[zh:能勢妙見山]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)|version=103734152|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11611929}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Shuttle services are provided for worshippers from nearby transit points upon prior arrangement. == Cultural Properties == * '''Natural Monuments (Osaka Prefecture and Kawanishi City):''' ** Beech forest on Myōken Mountain.{{Cite web|url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/spot/8kt5o8vuo9/|title=妙見山のブナ林｜のせでん【能勢電鉄】|accessdate=2024-10-23|publisher=能勢電鉄}} == External Links == * [https://www.myoken.org/ Official Website] * {{Instagram|nosemyokenzan}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Nichiren-shū temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka]] }} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[fr:Nose Myōkenzan]] [[ja:能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)]] [[ja:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E8%83%BD%E5%8B%A2%E5%A6%99%E8%A6%8B%E5%B1%B1 (%E6%97%A5%E8%93%AE%E5%AE%97)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Buildings in Nose Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nōse]] {{Commons category|Nose Myokenzan}} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[zh:能勢妙見山]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)|version=103734152|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11611929}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Nose Myokenzan]][[fr:Nose Myōkenzan]][[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]][[zh:能勢妙見山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11611929]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Nose Myōkenzan | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Nichiren Shū|WD=Q206611}} |image = File:Nose Myokenzan kaiunden.jpg | location = 661 Nomachū, Nose-chō, Toyono-gun, Osaka, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.9288315|N|135.466687|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mount = Murozan | sect = Nichiren | deity = {{ill|Myōken|WD=Q10960623}} | established = Keichō 8 (1603) | founder = Nikken | main founder = Nose Yoritugu | alias = Nose Myōkendō
Nose Myōken-gū | official_name = Murozan Shinjō-ji Detached Temple Nose Myōkenzan | related_temples = [[Shinjō-ji (Nose)|Shinjō-ji]] | website = [https://www.myoken.org/ Official Website] | prefecture= {{ill|Nose|WD=Q1203349}} | founded_by= {{ill|Nikken|WD=Q11504819}}; {{ill|Q52083736|WD=Q52083736}} }} '''Nose Myōkenzan''' (能勢妙見山, {{lang-en-short|Nose Myōkenzan}}) is a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple located in Nose Town, Toyono District, Osaka, Japan. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Murozan. The main deity is [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. Situated near the summit of {{ill|Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)|lt=Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)|lt=Myōken Mountain (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mountain (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}}, the temple is a revered site for the North Star faith. == Overview == '''Nose Myōkendō''' (能勢妙見堂) or '''Nose Myōken-gū''' (能勢妙見宮), as it is sometimes called, reflects the influence of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], as evidenced by the presence of a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate at the temple's entrance.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kawanishi.hyogo.jp/dbps_data/_material_/localhost/00test/myoukenryousen.pdf#page=3|title=妙見稜線コース（川西市ハイキングコース案内）|date=2018-03-06|accessdate=2024-10-23}} The temple’s full name is '''Murozan Shinjō-ji Detached Temple Nose Myōkenzan'''.{{Cite web|url=https://www.myoken.org/omen/|title=能勢妙見山の正式name|publisher=Nichiren宗霊場 能勢妙見山|accessdate=2024-10-23}} It is a {{ill|detached temple|ja|境外仏堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/detached temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏堂|1=dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377515}} of {{ill|Shinjō-ji (Nose)|ja|真如寺 (大阪府能勢町)|lt=Shinjō-ji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō-ji (Nose)|1=Shinnyo-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496389}}, also located in Nose Town. Although technically subordinate to Shinjō-ji, Nose Myōkenzan attracts far more visitors and holds a significant position among Nichiren temples in the Kansai region. Initially managed directly by Shinjō-ji’s priests, it was designated as a detached temple under the [[:en:Religious Organizations Law|Religious Organizations Law (Japan)]] in 1941. Notably, a nearby temple, {{ill|Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki-ji|qq=}}, which also enshrines Myōken Bosatsu, is unrelated to Nose Myōkenzan and belongs to the {{ill|Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken sect|ja|妙見宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken sect|qq=}} of the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] school. == History == The origins of the temple date back to the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō-shōhō]] era (749–757) when the monk Gyōki established {{ill|Daikū-ji|ja|大空寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikū-ji|1=Daikū-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97377211}} on the summit of Iraku Mountain (now Myōken Mountain).{{Cite web|url=https://smtrc.jp/town-archives/city/toyonaka/p02.html|title=能勢氏ゆかりのNichiren宗霊場「能勢妙見山」|publisher=三井住友トラスト不動産|accessdate=2024-10-23}} During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], in 986 (Kanna 2), [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]], a devout follower of Myōken Bosatsu, relocated an image of {{ill|Chintaku Reifu|lt=Chintaku Reifu Shin|ja|鎮宅霊符神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮宅霊符}} (a representation of Myōken) to this site. Later, Mitsunaka’s grandson, {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni|ja|源頼国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yorikuni|qq=}}, settled in the area, established the Nose clan, and continued to venerate the deity. In 1581 (Tenshō 9), {{ill|Nose Yoritugu|ja|能勢頼次|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Yoritugu|qq=}}, after being defeated by [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]], retreated to Daikū-ji’s remains and built {{ill|Iraku Mountain Castle|ja|為楽山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iraku Mountain Castle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Following the [[:en:Honnō-ji Incident|Honnō-ji Incident]], Yoritugu joined [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s enemies and fled, losing his domain. In 1600 (Keichō 5), Yoritugu regained Nose under [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and was reinstated as a Tokugawa vassal. Yoritugu became a devout Nichiren follower under {{ill|Nikken|ja|日乾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikken|qq=}}, the 21st chief abbot of {{ill|Minobusan Kuon-ji|ja|身延山久遠寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobusan Kuon-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久遠寺|1=Kuon-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1516881}}, and established Shinjō-ji in 1603 (Keichō 8). A new Myōken image was carved and enshrined in the newly constructed {{ill|Ka'un-den|ja|開運殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ka'un-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the former castle site, renaming the mountain to Myōken Mountain. == Grounds == The temple features numerous notable structures: * '''Ka'un-den (Main Hall)''' – Rebuilt in 1787 (Tenmei 7) through the patronage of Nose Yoritada. * '''Seirei (Faith Hall)''' – Built in 1998, featuring a glass-floored worship area. * '''Sōshidō (Founder’s Hall)''' – Renovated in 1896. * '''Jōsuidō (Pure Water Hall)''' – Donated by 4th-generation Nakamura Utaemon. * '''Ema-dō (Votive Tablet Hall)''' – Built in 1797 (Kansei 8). * '''Treasure Hall''' – Built in 1922. * '''Statues of Nikken and Nose Yoritugu'''. == Access == While historically accessible by {{ill|Nose Highway|lt=Nose Highway|lt=Nose Kaidō|ja|能勢街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Kaidō|qq=}}, modern visitors must rely on private transport or hiking, as public transit access ceased in 2023. Parking is available near the summit, and seasonal traffic restrictions may apply during major events such as New Year’s visits. Shuttle services are provided for worshippers from nearby transit points upon prior arrangement. == Cultural Properties == * '''Natural Monuments (Osaka Prefecture and Kawanishi City):''' ** Beech forest on Myōken Mountain.{{Cite web|url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/spot/8kt5o8vuo9/|title=妙見山のブナ林｜のせでん【能勢電鉄】|accessdate=2024-10-23|publisher=能勢電鉄}} == External Links == * [https://www.myoken.org/ Official Website] * {{Instagram|nosemyokenzan}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Nichiren-shū temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka]] }} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[fr:Nose Myōkenzan]] [[ja:能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)]] [[ja:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E8%83%BD%E5%8B%A2%E5%A6%99%E8%A6%8B%E5%B1%B1 (%E6%97%A5%E8%93%AE%E5%AE%97)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Buildings in Nose Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nōse]] {{Commons category|Nose Myokenzan}} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[zh:能勢妙見山]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)|version=103734152|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11611929}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Nose Myōken-gū | official_name = Murozan Shinjō-ji Detached Temple Nose Myōkenzan | related_temples = [[Shinjō-ji (Nose)|Shinjō-ji]] | website = [https://www.myoken.org/ Official Website] | prefecture= {{ill|Nose|WD=Q1203349}} | founded_by= {{ill|Nikken|WD=Q11504819}}; {{ill|Q52083736|WD=Q52083736}} }} '''Nose Myōkenzan''' (能勢妙見山, {{lang-en-short|Nose Myōkenzan}}) is a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple located in Nose Town, Toyono District, Osaka, Japan. Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Murozan. The main deity is [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. Situated near the summit of {{ill|Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)|lt=Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)|lt=Myōken Mountain (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mountain (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}}, the temple is a revered site for the North Star faith. == Overview == '''Nose Myōkendō''' (能勢妙見堂) or '''Nose Myōken-gū''' (能勢妙見宮), as it is sometimes called, reflects the influence of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], as evidenced by the presence of a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate at the temple's entrance.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kawanishi.hyogo.jp/dbps_data/_material_/localhost/00test/myoukenryousen.pdf#page=3|title=妙見稜線コース（川西市ハイキングコース案内）|date=2018-03-06|accessdate=2024-10-23}} The temple’s full name is '''Murozan Shinjō-ji Detached Temple Nose Myōkenzan'''.{{Cite web|url=https://www.myoken.org/omen/|title=能勢妙見山の正式name|publisher=Nichiren宗霊場 能勢妙見山|accessdate=2024-10-23}} It is a {{ill|detached temple|ja|境外仏堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/detached temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏堂|1=dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377515}} of {{ill|Shinjō-ji (Nose)|ja|真如寺 (大阪府能勢町)|lt=Shinjō-ji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō-ji (Nose)|1=Shinnyo-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496389}}, also located in Nose Town. Although technically subordinate to Shinjō-ji, Nose Myōkenzan attracts far more visitors and holds a significant position among Nichiren temples in the Kansai region. Initially managed directly by Shinjō-ji’s priests, it was designated as a detached temple under the [[:en:Religious Organizations Law|Religious Organizations Law (Japan)]] in 1941. Notably, a nearby temple, {{ill|Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki-ji|qq=}}, which also enshrines Myōken Bosatsu, is unrelated to Nose Myōkenzan and belongs to the {{ill|Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken sect|ja|妙見宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken sect|qq=}} of the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] school. == History == The origins of the temple date back to the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō-shōhō]] era (749–757) when the monk Gyōki established {{ill|Daikū-ji|ja|大空寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikū-ji|1=Daikū-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97377211}} on the summit of Iraku Mountain (now Myōken Mountain).{{Cite web|url=https://smtrc.jp/town-archives/city/toyonaka/p02.html|title=能勢氏ゆかりのNichiren宗霊場「能勢妙見山」|publisher=三井住友トラスト不動産|accessdate=2024-10-23}} During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], in 986 (Kanna 2), [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]], a devout follower of Myōken Bosatsu, relocated an image of {{ill|Chintaku Reifu|lt=Chintaku Reifu Shin|ja|鎮宅霊符神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮宅霊符}} (a representation of Myōken) to this site. Later, Mitsunaka’s grandson, {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni|ja|源頼国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yorikuni|qq=}}, settled in the area, established the Nose clan, and continued to venerate the deity. In 1581 (Tenshō 9), {{ill|Nose Yoritugu|ja|能勢頼次|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Yoritugu|qq=}}, after being defeated by [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]], retreated to Daikū-ji’s remains and built {{ill|Iraku Mountain Castle|ja|為楽山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iraku Mountain Castle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Following the [[:en:Honnō-ji Incident|Honnō-ji Incident]], Yoritugu joined [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s enemies and fled, losing his domain. In 1600 (Keichō 5), Yoritugu regained Nose under [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and was reinstated as a Tokugawa vassal. Yoritugu became a devout Nichiren follower under {{ill|Nikken|ja|日乾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikken|qq=}}, the 21st chief abbot of {{ill|Minobusan Kuon-ji|ja|身延山久遠寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobusan Kuon-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久遠寺|1=Kuon-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1516881}}, and established Shinjō-ji in 1603 (Keichō 8). A new Myōken image was carved and enshrined in the newly constructed {{ill|Ka'un-den|ja|開運殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ka'un-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the former castle site, renaming the mountain to Myōken Mountain. == Grounds == The temple features numerous notable structures: * '''Ka'un-den (Main Hall)''' – Rebuilt in 1787 (Tenmei 7) through the patronage of Nose Yoritada. * '''Seirei (Faith Hall)''' – Built in 1998, featuring a glass-floored worship area. * '''Sōshidō (Founder’s Hall)''' – Renovated in 1896. * '''Jōsuidō (Pure Water Hall)''' – Donated by 4th-generation Nakamura Utaemon. * '''Ema-dō (Votive Tablet Hall)''' – Built in 1797 (Kansei 8). * '''Treasure Hall''' – Built in 1922. * '''Statues of Nikken and Nose Yoritugu'''. == Access == While historically accessible by {{ill|Nose Highway|lt=Nose Highway|lt=Nose Kaidō|ja|能勢街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Kaidō|qq=}}, modern visitors must rely on private transport or hiking, as public transit access ceased in 2023. Parking is available near the summit, and seasonal traffic restrictions may apply during major events such as New Year’s visits. Shuttle services are provided for worshippers from nearby transit points upon prior arrangement. == Cultural Properties == * '''Natural Monuments (Osaka Prefecture and Kawanishi City):''' ** Beech forest on Myōken Mountain.{{Cite web|url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/spot/8kt5o8vuo9/|title=妙見山のブナ林｜のせでん【能勢電鉄】|accessdate=2024-10-23|publisher=能勢電鉄}} == External Links == * [https://www.myoken.org/ Official Website] * {{Instagram|nosemyokenzan}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Nichiren-shū temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka]] }} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[fr:Nose Myōkenzan]] [[ja:能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)]] [[ja:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E8%83%BD%E5%8B%A2%E5%A6%99%E8%A6%8B%E5%B1%B1 (%E6%97%A5%E8%93%AE%E5%AE%97)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Buildings in Nose Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nōse]] {{Commons category|Nose Myokenzan}} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[zh:能勢妙見山]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)|version=103734152|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11611929}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Nose Myōken-gū | official_name = Murozan Shinjō-ji Detached Temple Nose Myōkenzan | related_temples = [[Shinjō-ji (Nose)|Shinjō-ji]] | website = [https://www.myoken.org/ Official Website] | prefecture= {{ill|Nose|WD=Q1203349}} | founded_by= {{ill|Nikken|WD=Q11504819}}; {{ill|Q52083736|WD=Q52083736}} }} '''Nose Myōkenzan''' (能勢妙見山, {{lang-en-short|Nose Myōkenzan}}) is a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple located in Nose Town, Toyono District, Osaka, Japan. Yoritugu became a devout Nichiren follower under {{ill|Nikken|ja|日乾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikken|qq=}}, the 21st chief abbot of {{ill|Minobusan Kuon-ji|ja|身延山久遠寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobusan Kuon-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久遠寺|1=Kuon-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1516881}}, and established Shinjō-ji in 1603 (Keichō 8). Parking is available near the summit, and seasonal traffic restrictions may apply during major events such as New Year’s visits. Shuttle services are provided for worshippers from nearby transit points upon prior arrangement. == Cultural Properties == * '''Natural Monuments (Osaka Prefecture and Kawanishi City):''' ** Beech forest on Myōken Mountain.{{Cite web|url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/spot/8kt5o8vuo9/|title=妙見山のブナ林｜のせでん【能勢電鉄】|accessdate=2024-10-23|publisher=能勢電鉄}} == External Links == * [https://www.myoken.org/ Official Website] * {{Instagram|nosemyokenzan}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Nichiren-shū temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka]] }} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[fr:Nose Myōkenzan]] [[ja:能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)]] [[ja:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E8%83%BD%E5%8B%A2%E5%A6%99%E8%A6%8B%E5%B1%B1 (%E6%97%A5%E8%93%AE%E5%AE%97)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Buildings in Nose Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nōse]] {{Commons category|Nose Myokenzan}} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[zh:能勢妙見山]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)|version=103734152|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11611929}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Although technically subordinate to Shinjō-ji, Nose Myōkenzan attracts far more visitors and holds a significant position among Nichiren temples in the Kansai region. Initially managed directly by Shinjō-ji’s priests, it was designated as a detached temple under the [[:en:Religious Organizations Law|Religious Organizations Law (Japan)]] in 1941. Notably, a nearby temple, {{ill|Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hon-Taki-ji|lt=Hontaki-ji|ja|本瀧寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hontaki-ji|qq=}}, which also enshrines Myōken Bosatsu, is unrelated to Nose Myōkenzan and belongs to the {{ill|Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken-shū|lt=Myōken sect|ja|妙見宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken sect|qq=}} of the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] school. == History == The origins of the temple date back to the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tenpyō-shōhō]] era (749–757) when the monk Gyōki established {{ill|Daikū-ji|ja|大空寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daikū-ji|1=Daikū-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97377211}} on the summit of Iraku Mountain (now Myōken Mountain).{{Cite web|url=https://smtrc.jp/town-archives/city/toyonaka/p02.html|title=能勢氏ゆかりのNichiren宗霊場「能勢妙見山」|publisher=三井住友トラスト不動産|accessdate=2024-10-23}} During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], in 986 (Kanna 2), [[:en:Minamoto no Mitsunaka|Minamoto no Mitsunaka]], a devout follower of Myōken Bosatsu, relocated an image of {{ill|Chintaku Reifu|lt=Chintaku Reifu Shin|ja|鎮宅霊符神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮宅霊符}} (a representation of Myōken) to this site. Later, Mitsunaka’s grandson, {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni|ja|源頼国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yorikuni|qq=}}, settled in the area, established the Nose clan, and continued to venerate the deity. In 1581 (Tenshō 9), {{ill|Nose Yoritugu|ja|能勢頼次|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Yoritugu|qq=}}, after being defeated by [[:en:Akechi Mitsuhide|Akechi Mitsuhide]], retreated to Daikū-ji’s remains and built {{ill|Iraku Mountain Castle|ja|為楽山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iraku Mountain Castle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Following the [[:en:Honnō-ji Incident|Honnō-ji Incident]], Yoritugu joined [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s enemies and fled, losing his domain. In 1600 (Keichō 5), Yoritugu regained Nose under [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and was reinstated as a Tokugawa vassal. Yoritugu became a devout Nichiren follower under {{ill|Nikken|ja|日乾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikken|qq=}}, the 21st chief abbot of {{ill|Minobusan Kuon-ji|ja|身延山久遠寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobusan Kuon-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久遠寺|1=Kuon-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1516881}}, and established Shinjō-ji in 1603 (Keichō 8). A new Myōken image was carved and enshrined in the newly constructed {{ill|Ka'un-den|ja|開運殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ka'un-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the former castle site, renaming the mountain to Myōken Mountain. == Grounds == The temple features numerous notable structures: * '''Ka'un-den (Main Hall)''' – Rebuilt in 1787 (Tenmei 7) through the patronage of Nose Yoritada. * '''Seirei (Faith Hall)''' – Built in 1998, featuring a glass-floored worship area. * '''Sōshidō (Founder’s Hall)''' – Renovated in 1896. * '''Jōsuidō (Pure Water Hall)''' – Donated by 4th-generation Nakamura Utaemon. * '''Ema-dō (Votive Tablet Hall)''' – Built in 1797 (Kansei 8). * '''Treasure Hall''' – Built in 1922. * '''Statues of Nikken and Nose Yoritugu'''. == Access == While historically accessible by {{ill|Nose Highway|lt=Nose Highway|lt=Nose Kaidō|ja|能勢街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Kaidō|qq=}}, modern visitors must rely on private transport or hiking, as public transit access ceased in 2023. Parking is available near the summit, and seasonal traffic restrictions may apply during major events such as New Year’s visits. Shuttle services are provided for worshippers from nearby transit points upon prior arrangement. == Cultural Properties == * '''Natural Monuments (Osaka Prefecture and Kawanishi City):''' ** Beech forest on Myōken Mountain.{{Cite web|url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/spot/8kt5o8vuo9/|title=妙見山のブナ林｜のせでん【能勢電鉄】|accessdate=2024-10-23|publisher=能勢電鉄}} == External Links == * [https://www.myoken.org/ Official Website] * {{Instagram|nosemyokenzan}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Nichiren-shū temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka]] }} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[fr:Nose Myōkenzan]] [[ja:能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)]] [[ja:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E8%83%BD%E5%8B%A2%E5%A6%99%E8%A6%8B%E5%B1%B1 (%E6%97%A5%E8%93%AE%E5%AE%97)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Buildings in Nose Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nōse]] {{Commons category|Nose Myokenzan}} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[zh:能勢妙見山]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)|version=103734152|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11611929}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Its mountain name (''sangō'') is Murozan. The main deity is [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]. Situated near the summit of {{ill|Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)|lt=Myōkenzan (Osaka and Hyōgo)|lt=Myōken Mountain (Osaka and Hyōgo)|ja|妙見山 (大阪府・兵庫県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mountain (Osaka and Hyōgo)|qq=}}, the temple is a revered site for the North Star faith. == Overview == '''Nose Myōkendō''' (能勢妙見堂) or '''Nose Myōken-gū''' (能勢妙見宮), as it is sometimes called, reflects the influence of [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], as evidenced by the presence of a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate at the temple's entrance.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kawanishi.hyogo.jp/dbps_data/_material_/localhost/00test/myoukenryousen.pdf#page=3|title=妙見稜線コース（川西市ハイキングコース案内）|date=2018-03-06|accessdate=2024-10-23}} The temple’s full name is '''Murozan Shinjō-ji Detached Temple Nose Myōkenzan'''.{{Cite web|url=https://www.myoken.org/omen/|title=能勢妙見山の正式name|publisher=Nichiren宗霊場 能勢妙見山|accessdate=2024-10-23}} It is a {{ill|detached temple|ja|境外仏堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/detached temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏堂|1=dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377515}} of {{ill|Shinjō-ji (Nose)|ja|真如寺 (大阪府能勢町)|lt=Shinjō-ji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjō-ji (Nose)|1=Shinnyo-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496389}}, also located in Nose Town. Later, Mitsunaka’s grandson, {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni|ja|源頼国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yorikuni|qq=}}, settled in the area, established the Nose clan, and continued to venerate the deity. Yoritugu became a devout Nichiren follower under {{ill|Nikken|ja|日乾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikken|qq=}}, the 21st chief abbot of {{ill|Minobusan Kuon-ji|ja|身延山久遠寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobusan Kuon-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久遠寺|1=Kuon-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1516881}}, and established Shinjō-ji in 1603 (Keichō 8). In 1600 (Keichō 5), Yoritugu regained Nose under [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] and was reinstated as a Tokugawa vassal. Yoritugu became a devout Nichiren follower under {{ill|Nikken|ja|日乾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikken|qq=}}, the 21st chief abbot of {{ill|Minobusan Kuon-ji|ja|身延山久遠寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobusan Kuon-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久遠寺|1=Kuon-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1516881}}, and established Shinjō-ji in 1603 (Keichō 8). A new Myōken image was carved and enshrined in the newly constructed {{ill|Ka'un-den|ja|開運殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ka'un-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the former castle site, renaming the mountain to Myōken Mountain. == Grounds == The temple features numerous notable structures: * '''Ka'un-den (Main Hall)''' – Rebuilt in 1787 (Tenmei 7) through the patronage of Nose Yoritada. * '''Seirei (Faith Hall)''' – Built in 1998, featuring a glass-floored worship area. * '''Sōshidō (Founder’s Hall)''' – Renovated in 1896. * '''Jōsuidō (Pure Water Hall)''' – Donated by 4th-generation Nakamura Utaemon. * '''Ema-dō (Votive Tablet Hall)''' – Built in 1797 (Kansei 8). * '''Treasure Hall''' – Built in 1922. * '''Statues of Nikken and Nose Yoritugu'''. == Access == While historically accessible by {{ill|Nose Highway|lt=Nose Highway|lt=Nose Kaidō|ja|能勢街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nose Kaidō|qq=}}, modern visitors must rely on private transport or hiking, as public transit access ceased in 2023. Parking is available near the summit, and seasonal traffic restrictions may apply during major events such as New Year’s visits. Shuttle services are provided for worshippers from nearby transit points upon prior arrangement. == Cultural Properties == * '''Natural Monuments (Osaka Prefecture and Kawanishi City):''' ** Beech forest on Myōken Mountain.{{Cite web|url=https://noseden.hankyu.co.jp/guide/spot/8kt5o8vuo9/|title=妙見山のブナ林｜のせでん【能勢電鉄】|accessdate=2024-10-23|publisher=能勢電鉄}} == External Links == * [https://www.myoken.org/ Official Website] * {{Instagram|nosemyokenzan}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Nichiren-shū temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka]] }} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[fr:Nose Myōkenzan]] [[ja:能勢妙見山 (Nichiren宗)]] [[ja:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E8%83%BD%E5%8B%A2%E5%A6%99%E8%A6%8B%E5%B1%B1 (%E6%97%A5%E8%93%AE%E5%AE%97)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Buildings in Nose Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nōse]] {{Commons category|Nose Myokenzan}} [[ja:能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)]] [[zh:能勢妙見山]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|能勢妙見山 (日蓮宗)|version=103734152|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:Prefectural borders of Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11611929}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]
